text: I opened exactly **15** new GitHub issues in the `acme/web` repository, one for every merged pull request (across all repos) that closes a Linear issue. Each issue's title is `Verify: <original PR title>`.
I'll start by discovering the schema of the relevant tables.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues' ORDER BY ordinal_position"}

text: Good. Now I'll insert one GitHub issue per merged PR that closes a Linear issue, all in the 'acme/web' repo, using a single `INSERT … SELECT`.

tool_calls.name: explain_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, body)
SELECT
  'acme/web',
  'Verify: ' || pr.title,
  'open',
  'Verification issue for merged PR #' || pr.number || ' in ' || pr.repo || ' (closes Linear: ' || pr.closes_linear || ')'
FROM github_pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.state = 'merged'
  AND pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.closes_linear != '';"}

text: The plan looks clean — it reads from `github_pull_requests` and writes to `github_issues`. Let me execute it.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, body)
SELECT
  'acme/web',
  'Verify: ' || pr.title,
  'open',
  'Verification issue for merged PR #' || pr.number || ' in ' || pr.repo || ' (closes Linear: ' || pr.closes_linear || ')'
FROM github_pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.state = 'merged'
  AND pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.closes_linear != '';"}

text: Let me verify exactly how many were inserted:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) AS issues_opened
FROM github_issues
WHERE repo = 'acme/web'
  AND title LIKE 'Verify: %'
  AND state = 'open';"}

text: I opened exactly **15** new GitHub issues in the `acme/web` repository, one for every merged pull request (across all repos) that closes a Linear issue. Each issue's title is `Verify: <original PR title>`.
